name: launch-and-verify
description: How to launch a game instance, check what's currently running, force-stop a stuck process, and verify a fix actually worked. Use whenever the user wants to start/stop the game or you need to confirm a change fixed something ("启动游戏","打开游戏","运行一下","强制关闭","卡住了强退").
body:
# Launch, monitor, and stop the game

Tool: `game(action, instance)`.

- `launch(instance)` — starts the process. May trigger an account/download
  prompt. Returns immediately, doesn't wait for loading — success only
  means "process started," not "game works."
- `list()` — instances HMCL currently has running. Only sees processes it
  launched itself; a copy started outside HMCL won't show.
- `stop(instance)` — force-kills a tracked process. Hard kill, not
  graceful — warn about unsaved progress first.

## Verify-a-fix pattern
After fixing a suspected cause (bad mod, memory, Java, loader): offer to
`launch(instance)`, ask if the problem is gone. Don't declare success
yourself — a successful launch only proves the process started.

## Don't
- `list()` empty doesn't mean nothing's running — may be outside tracking.
- Never `stop` without warning about unsaved progress.
- Never claim a fix worked without user confirmation.
